title: Dependency/supply-chain audit + SRI hardening
context: As a static site with no backend, repair-cafe-kits has a small but real supply-chain risk in its npm dependencies. Pinned/locked dependencies via the pnpm lockfile reduce drift, but a periodic audit catches known vulnerabilities and unnecessary transitive dependencies. Subresource Integrity (SRI) hashes harden any external resource references that may exist (e.g. if any CDN assets are referenced). This is a recurring maintenance task to be run at least annually or after any significant dependency update.
objective: Audit all npm dependencies for known vulnerabilities and unnecessary packages; update or replace problematic dependencies; add SRI hashes to any external resource references where applicable; confirm the pnpm lockfile is up to date.
acceptanceCriteria: pnpm audit (or equivalent) is run and all high/critical severity vulnerabilities are resolved (updated, replaced, or documented as accepted risk with justification)
Unnecessary or redundant dependencies are identified and removed where feasible; the lockfile is updated and committed
Any external resource references in the built HTML/CSS (e.g. CDN scripts or stylesheets) have SRI hashes added; if none exist, this is confirmed and documented
The no-telemetry/PII audit (static + runtime E2E) still passes after dependency updates; pnpm build && pnpm test && pnpm lint pass
A brief audit report is committed to the repo recording: vulnerabilities found/resolved, dependencies updated, SRI changes made, and the date of the audit
output: A merged PR with resolved dependency vulnerabilities, an updated lockfile, SRI hashes on any external resource references, and a committed audit report.
